senses.gloss: to see (to perceive with the eyes)
to see again in one's mind, to play back a memory, to look back on
to deem true, to opine or hold the opinion of, to have that outlook on something, to see or view it that way, to be of that thinking or to have such thoughts

examples.text: فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ مَعَهُ ٱلسَّعۡىَ قَالَ يَـٰـبُنَىَّ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰ فِى ٱلۡمَنَامِ أَنِّىٓ أَذۡبَحُكَ فَٱنۡظُرۡ مَاذَا تَرَىٰ قَالَ يَـٰٓـأَبَتِ ٱفۡعَلۡ مَا تُؤۡمَرُ سَتَجِدُنِىٓ إِن شَاءَ ٱللهُ مِنَ ٱلصَّابِرِينَ
examples.english: "Then when the boy reached the age to work with him, Abraham said, “O my dear son! I have seen in a dream that I ˹must˺ sacrifice you. So tell me what you think.” He replied, “O my dear father! Do as you are commanded. Allah willing, you will find me steadfast.”"

senses.gloss: to review in one's mind, to consider it, to reflect on it, to contemplate, to review something to see its deeper significance, to look back over, to speculate upon
to plant a flag or banner in the ground, to place down one's ensign or mark
to hit someone's lung, to complain of pain in one's lungs
to dream, to have a dream
to imagine, to fancy, to conceive a thought or to conceptualize, to see (to perceive in one's dream)
to envision, to foresee, to suppose, to anticipate a future outcome, to speculate or reckon it will be, to have a premonition or forewarning

etymology.text: Cognate with Biblical Hebrew רָאָה (rāʾâ) and Ge'ez ርእየ (rəʾyä).
